qa_pairs.question: How can I determine the exact version of my SSH client on OS X?
qa_pairs.answer: You can determine the exact version of your SSH client on OS X using the command `ssh -V`.

qa_pairs.question: Which OS X SSH clients are compatible with the new configuration?
qa_pairs.answer: Compatible OS X SSH clients include OpenSSH 7.4p1 with OpenSSL 1.0.2k (Homebrew) and OpenSSH 7.9p1 with LibreSSL 2.7.3 (OS X 10.14.5).

qa_pairs.question: What versions of OpenSSH are compatible on OS X?
qa_pairs.answer: OpenSSH 7.4p1 (Homebrew) and OpenSSH 7.9p1 (OS X 10.14.5) are compatible on OS X.

qa_pairs.question: Which Windows SSH clients are compatible with the new configuration?
qa_pairs.answer: Compatible Windows SSH clients include MobaXterm Home Edition v11.1, PuTTY 0.72, and Windows Services for Linux (WSL) v1, specifically Ubuntu 18.04 (OpenSSH_7.6p1, OpenSSL 1.0.2n).

qa_pairs.question: Is MobaXterm compatible, and if so, which version?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, MobaXterm Home Edition v11.1 is compatible.

qa_pairs.question: Is PuTTY compatible, and if so, which version?
Which Windows Services for Linux (WSL) versions are compatible?
qa_pairs.answer: Windows Services for Linux (WSL) v1 is compatible, including Ubuntu 18.04 (OpenSSH_7.6p1, OpenSSL 1.0.2n).
